lesson: Year-End Final — Last Review & Exam Logistics
banner: Week 38 · Year-End Final — Last Review & Logistics
essential_question: What's left to firm up, and what do you need in place before the exam?
objective: Take one last mixed pass across all three units, then confirm the logistics — materials, timing, and the independent-work rule — for the MUS Final Exam.

agenda.title: Warm-Up — Two Weeks of Review Behind You

content.prompt: You've reviewed all three units over the last two weeks. What's the one topic you still want to see one more example of before tomorrow's exam?

content.heading: A problem from each unit, plus a wildcard
Materials and the independent-work rule
content.note: The Final Exam is completed independently — no help from Colton, an answer key, or an AI tool while the test is in progress.

content.preview: Next session: the MUS Final Exam itself — independent, no help.

success_criteria.text: I can solve a mixed cumulative problem from any of the three units without help
I can confirm I have the materials and understand the independent-work rule for the Final Exam

questions.prompt: A pair of supplementary angles sums to…
Find the area of a trapezoid with parallel sides 6 and 10 and height 4.
A cone has radius 3 and height 8. Find its volume in terms of π.
questions.explain: Volume of a cone = ⅓ × π × r² × h.

questions.prompt: In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is 10 and one leg is 6. Find the tangent of the angle opposite the leg of length 6.
questions.explain: Find the missing leg with the Pythagorean theorem first, then tangent = opposite/adjacent.

questions.prompt: List everything you need for the Final Exam — your Test Booklet, pencil, ruler, protractor, and anything else. Confirm you have each.
The Final Exam is completed independently — no help from Colton, an answer key, or an AI tool. If you get stuck mid-test, what should you do?
questions.answer: Stop and flag it for the debrief next session — don't look it up or ask for help mid-test.
questions.explain: This mirrors the course's assessment-integrity rule for all Lesson Tests, Unit Tests, and the Final Exam.

questions.prompt: Exit: on a scale that's honest, not hopeful — how ready do you feel for the Final, and what's the one thing you'd still like explained?
